title: Stapelia halipedicola
intro: Stapelia halipedicola, commonly known as the 'Carrion Flower', is a species of flowering succulent plant native to Namibia and South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in wound healing and immune system support. Bioactive constituents of Stapelia halipedicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including capsules, tinctures, and ointments, to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
taxonomy: Stapelia halipedicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to South Africa and belongs to the genus Stapelia.
